sections.subtitle: Team Form and League Standings
sections.text: Athletic Club, sitting at 4th place in the league with 61 points, are in a strong position as they host this match at San Mamés in Bilbao. In contrast, Deportivo Alavés are struggling in 17th place with only 35 points, fighting to avoid relegation. Athletic Club have not secured a win in their last 3 home matches, which might give Alavés a glimmer of hope. However, Alavés have managed to avoid defeat in their last 3 away games, showing some resilience on the road. This matchup pits a top-tier side against a relegation-threatened team, making it a crucial encounter for both.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Athletic Club have dominated with 3 wins and 2 draws, leaving Alavés without a victory in this fixture. Their most recent encounter on December 15, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw, with Nahuel Tenaglia earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.6 Sofascore rating. Athletic Club have not lost to Alavés in their last 8 meetings, showcasing their historical edge. Moreover, Athletic have been the first to score and the first-half winners in their last 5 head-to-head games, indicating their tendency to start strong against this opponent. Interestingly, 9 out of 9 of these matches have seen less than 2.5 goals, suggesting a typically low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Athletic Club
sections.text: Despite missing key midfielder Oihan Sancet, who has scored 15 goals this season with an impressive xG overperformance of 5.66, Athletic Club still boast talent in their lineup. Another notable absence is Iñaki Williams, whose 8 assists place him 3rd in the league for assists, alongside Nico Williams, ranked 5th for successful dribbles per game at 2.3. In their absence, players like Gorka Guruzeta, with 6 goals and 2 assists, will be crucial up front. Defender Daniel Vivian, with a solid average rating of 7.12 and 3 goals, will aim to anchor the backline. Additionally, Unai Simón has been a standout in past matches against Alavés, averaging 3.2 saves per game and a 7.44 Sofascore rating over the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Deportivo Alavés
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés will rely heavily on forward Kike García, who has netted 12 goals this season and overperformed his xG by 3.57, making him a significant threat. Midfielder Carlos Vicente, with 5 assists and 4 goals, will look to create chances alongside Joan Jordán, who also has 4 goals and a strong average rating of 7.07. Nahuel Tenaglia, recently in form with a 7.5 average Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, will be pivotal in defense after his standout performance in their last meeting. However, the absence of defender Abdelkabir Abqar, known for his long-ball distribution with a league rank of 5th at 9%, could weaken their defensive structure.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Athletic Club lead the league in headers scored with 12 and have the best defensive record, conceding only 26 goals, ranked 1st for least goals conceded. They also excel in interceptions per game at 9.06, ranked 1st, and possessions won in the final third at 4.62 per game, ranked 2nd, indicating strong pressing. Their goal distribution shows late-game strength, with 30.77% of goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Meanwhile, Alavés also peak late, scoring 30.43% of their goals in the same 76th-90th minute period, which could make for an exciting finish. However, Alavés matches have seen less than 2.5 goals in their last 6 away games, aligning with the low-scoring trend in head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: With referee José Luis Munuera Montero officiating, known for averaging 4.7 yellow cards per game, expect a potentially feisty encounter. Alavés matches have seen more than 4.5 cards in 7 of their last 8 away games, pointing to a high likelihood of bookings. Athletic Club, with only 60 yellow cards this season (ranked 3rd for least yellows), might be more disciplined, averaging 1.76 yellows per game. In terms of set-pieces, Athletic average 5.12 corners per game (ranked 5th), but recent trends show less than 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 7 home games and 4 of Alavés’ last 5 away games, suggesting a moderate number of corners.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Home Advantage
sections.text: Playing at San Mamés in Bilbao, Athletic Club have a significant home advantage, bolstered by their passionate fanbase and familiarity with the pitch. Their defensive solidity, conceding the fewest goals in the league at 26, could be even more pronounced at home. Alavés, however, have struggled to keep clean sheets in their last 5 away games against Athletic, which could play into the hosts’ hands. The venue has often been a fortress for Athletic, especially in this fixture, where they’ve maintained an unbeaten streak of 8 games against Alavés.
